justification: Wave 5C retro-apply of ECON-OOS1/OOS2 to pre-existing HY-IG v2 reference pair. Total sample = 312 months (2000-01-03 through 2025-12-31, daily parquet at data/hy_ig_v2_spy_daily_20260410.parquet). Formula under split_policy_id v1_max36_25pct_cap120 yields span_months = min(max(36, round(312 * 0.25)), 120) = min(max(36, 78), 120) = 78 months, which would produce oos_start = 2019-07-01, oos_end = 2025-12-31. However, the shipped winner_summary.json uses oos_period_start = 2018-01-01, oos_period_end = 2025-12-31 (96-month window), inherited verbatim from the original pre-ECON-OOS2 pipeline (reverse-engineered from oos_n = 2088 per the ECON-OOS2 Motivation paragraph — this is literally the exemplar the rule was written to regularize). Declared divergence per META-XVC: Prior method = reverse-inferred 96-month window (2018-01-01 start); New formula method = 78-month window (2019-07-01 start); Strong reason for KEEPING the 96-month window on this reference pair = (a) Wave 5C dispatch constraint is retro-apply of the record artifact, not re-running the tournament on a narrower window, which would invalidate the shipped winner_summary / tournament_results / portal; (b) all downstream agents (Ray narrative, Ace portal, Vera charts) are already pinned to the 2018-01-01 start and a silent re-window would cascade regressions; (c) the 96-month window is a legitimate supra-formula choice (4-year IS / 8-year OOS is defensible for a 25-year HY-IG history). Expected impact = none on shipped artifacts; the formula value is recorded here for traceability and future pairs will follow the formula strictly. Validation = formula computation shown above; next pair onboarded post-Wave-5C will use formula-derived dates with split_policy_id v1_max36_25pct_cap120. Cross-reference = regression_note_20260419.md Wave 5C retro-apply block.
